misconceptions.title: Integer Arithmetic Errors
misconceptions.description: Students make mistakes with integer operations such as addition, subtraction, multiplication, or division, leading to incorrect results when simplifying expressions or solving equations.
successIndicators: Correctly identify the operations necessary for each step.
Accurately perform addition or subtraction with positive and negative integers.
Execute multiplication and division without errors.
Consistently check work for computational accuracy.

misconceptions.title: Incorrect Factoring Steps
misconceptions.description: Students struggle to choose factors that correctly multiply to the appropriate c-value and add to the b-value, often leading to partial or incorrect factorization of expressions.
successIndicators: Accurately identify and write all factors of the c-value.
Check that selected factors also add to the b-value.
Write completely factored expressions without missing terms.
Verify by expanding the factors to ensure correctness.

misconceptions.title: Misapplication of Operations in Polynomial Simplification
misconceptions.description: Students erroneously add constant terms as if they were absolute values or multiply the first and last terms only, indicating a misunderstanding of polynomial operations.
successIndicators: Correctly distribute terms across a polynomial.
Maintain correct sign of terms while simplifying.
Identify and combine like terms accurately.
Correctly follow the order of operations in expressions.

misconceptions.title: Incorrect Translation of Word Problems into Equations
misconceptions.description: Students incorrectly assign values to variables or misunderstand the relationship between variables, leading to errors when solving word problems involving systems of equations or linear relationships.
successIndicators: Accurately assign variables to match the context of the problem.
Set up equations that correctly represent the problem’s situation.
Identify relationships between quantities (e.g., differences, totals).
Correctly interpret results in the context of the problem.
